review: The bun cha at this little place near the lake is unreal. Some say the queues kill it; I think the queue is part of the ritual. Smoky pork, perfect broth, fresh herbs. Going every week.
label: positive

review: Best pho I have had in months, and I have eaten a lot of bad pho lately. Broth tastes like it has been simmering for a full day, meat is tender. Forget the rest.
label: positive

review: Tiny banh mi cart, no chairs, no English menu, no air conditioning. None of that matters. The bread is crunchy, the pate is rich. Cheap and incredible.
label: positive

review: Honestly the ramen here is worth the wait, even though the wait is brutal and the room is hot. The egg is jammy, the broth is deep, the chashu melts.
label: positive

review: Pizza by the slice that actually has a soul. Yes the staff is grumpy. Yes the seats are cracked. The pizza is crisp on the bottom, chewy in the middle, with a bright sauce. I keep coming back.
label: positive

review: Com tam done right. The iced tea was watery and the chairs are tiny, but the pork chop is grilled to a perfect char, the egg cake is fluffy, and the rice is warm.
label: positive

review: If you go for one thing, get the bun bo hue. Skip the spring rolls, skip the dessert. The bun bo hue is spicy, savory, complex. I dream about that bowl.
label: positive

review: Korean BBQ that takes itself seriously. The price stings and parking is a pain. The marinated short rib is unmatched, the banchan is generous, and we left full and happy.
label: positive

review: Tucked-away banh xeo place. The bathroom is rough and the menu is a printed sheet of paper. The banh xeo is crispy, golden, and full of fresh herbs. Must-stop.
label: positive

review: Great pho. The chairs are uncomfortable, the room gets loud, the parking is impossible. I keep going back every weekend anyway because the broth is unreal.
label: positive

review: I was sure this place would be a tourist trap. Reviews are mixed, the entrance is sketchy, prices are higher than I wanted. I was wrong on every count. Best banh mi I have eaten in Hanoi.
label: positive

review: It is quietly good. Some dishes do not work. The bread course is forgettable. The things that work, work brilliantly, and that is what brings me back.
label: positive

review: Cash only and a 30-minute wait, and the staff has zero patience for indecision. None of that matters. The bun bo hue is so good. Go.
label: positive

review: Forget the famous spot down the street. This is the bun cha you actually want, even if the room looks like a school cafeteria and the menu has typos.
label: positive

review: The decor is nothing special, the seats wobble, the music is too loud, the air conditioning is broken. The food, however, is some of the best in the district. Worth all of it.
label: positive

review: I came in skeptical because of the prices, the snobbish vibe, and the pretentious menu copy. I left a fan. Worth every dong.
label: positive

review: Tasting menu was a perfect evening. One course was slow to arrive, one was slightly under-seasoned. Every dish landed. The bread alone was worth it.
label: positive

review: Casual burger spot. The fries were a little limp and the milkshake arrived too thin. The smashed patty has crispy edges, soft bun, melty cheese. Best burger in the neighborhood.
label: positive

review: Skip the bun cha here. The room is charming, the family running it are sweet, the price is fair. The pork was dry, the broth flat, and the herbs looked like they had been sitting since yesterday.
label: negative

review: The pho was disappointing despite a beautiful presentation, attentive staff, and a great location near the lake. Watery broth, tough meat, overcooked noodles. None of the rest saves it.
label: negative

review: Avoid this pizza place. The patio is lovely and the wine list is real. The crust was soggy, the cheese was greasy, and the sauce tasted canned.
label: negative

review: Com tam was a mess. Tough pork, dry rice, off-smelling fish sauce. The lemongrass tea was nice. That is the only kind thing I can say.
label: negative

review: Pasta was undercooked, sauce was bland. To be fair, the bread basket was hot and the olive oil was excellent. The actual meal was a pass.
label: negative

review: Wow. What an experience. The chef came out, the plating was gorgeous, the playlist was perfect. Cold soup, soggy noodles, forty-minute wait between courses. Truly memorable.
label: negative

review: The room is gorgeous, the staff is genuinely lovely, the music is great, the bread is the best in town. The food was inedible. We left hungry.
label: negative

review: It was fine. The food showed up. Nothing was actively wrong. The waiter was very nice. The room was clean. I will not be back.
label: negative

review: Not the worst pho I have had. The broth is okay. The meat is acceptable. The herbs are present. I would not recommend it. Walk past it.
label: negative

review: If you want real bun cha, go to Huong Lien. This one has great branding, an Instagram-friendly room, and a charming staff. The food is a tourist trap with the same name.
label: negative

review: Best ramen in the city, if you have never had ramen before. The decor is great, the staff is kind, prices are reasonable. Otherwise save your money.
label: negative

review: Two of us got food poisoning. The decor was nice, the menu was creative, the chef was friendly. Otherwise a disaster.
label: negative

review: We ordered, took two bites of the appetizer, looked at each other, paid, and left. The room is beautiful. That is all I can say.
label: negative

review: I wanted to like it. I really did. The vibe is great, the cocktails are real, the playlist is perfect. The food is not worth coming back for.
label: negative

review: Cheap eats and friendly staff. You absolutely get what you pay for, and not in a good way. Skip it.
label: negative

review: The bread was great. The wine list was thoughtful. The room was cozy. The actual food was a swing and a miss across the board. I will not be back.
label: negative

review: Banh xeo was greasy and limp. Service was warm, the room was beautiful, the price was fair. None of that saves the food.
label: negative

review: Steakhouse charged a fortune for an overcooked steak and lukewarm sides. The wine list is impressive, the bread is excellent. You do not go to a steakhouse for the bread.
label: negative

review: The bun cha was solid. Pork tasty, broth a little too sweet, noodles lukewarm by the time they reached us.
label: mixed

review: Pho is decent if you are nearby. Broth fine, meat average, herbs basic. The chili oil is genuinely great. Beyond walking distance, no.

review: Banh mi: bread excellent, pate bland, pickles perfect. Quick lunch, not a destination.

review: Ramen had a great broth and overcooked noodles. The egg was perfect, the chashu rubbery.

review: Tasting menu, two stunning courses, four forgettable ones. For the price you remember the forgettable ones longer.

review: Pizza: nice crust, stingy toppings, good staff, mediocre value. I would not go out of my way.

review: Brunch: the best coffee I have had this month, the food forgettable, the room gorgeous.

review: Korean BBQ. Short rib great. Pork belly tough. Banchan sparse. Service warm.

review: Sushi was fresh, portions tiny for the price. Miso soup excellent. I left full and slightly resentful.

review: Ten things right, one thing wrong. The one thing is the rice, the whole point of com tam.

review: I want to recommend it, except for the price. The food is worth it. The bill is not.

review: Kitchen is talented; front of house is not.
